item.content:encoded:
Well deserved! Though my updoot made it 301

Also this is one SCP that may have actually benefitted from having its image cc'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty bloody good, chap! I liked the interview log, a very fun read.
+1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is stated to be from here, but is no longer available to view. However, the site and its images are All Rights Reserved and would nevertheless be non-compliant with the wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: yay volgun
item.content:encoded: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ofvQ45po-k8

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: upvoted for hm becoming slightly more "british" with every word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very interesting. I like the flare gun plane sommoning concept. +1

item.title: Some minor details
item.content:encoded:
"Norwood" is named for https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Warren_Norwood . (note that the blackboxed surname is 6 characters long; note the date of death given for the Sergeant, note the dates of birth and death given for the author)

In the description of the supplies as not being of "functional utilitarian quality", ''utilitarian' is quite deliberately the wrong word.

item.title: Re: So, what about emergencies?
item.content:encoded: In my opinion, yes. However, any situation that has been pre-arranged by the Foundation does not count as an actual emergency.

item.title: So, what about emergencies?
item.content:encoded: If, in a hypothetical situation, a group of people was genuinely in an emergency, would the items dropped be useful?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really need to come to a decision on my cannon canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or, if it was bit bigger, between your cannon and this cannon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, now I have to choose between my canon and this canon. Great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an IRC log

<VAE> the phrase reusal remnds me of Eliza
<VAE> you know the speech bot
<Voct> I know what you mean
<Voct> not-quite-human AI doing translation
<Voct> Dr. Lionel ¦¦¦¦¦¦¦: I'm sorry to hear that. Our instruments show that you're reaching your exit point.
<Voct> Icarus' Wing: I am. It is quite cold. I cannot stop you from using the signal device, Doctor Lionel chap old bean. I politely ask, as one Englishman to another, that you stop wasting my time. I have supplies to deliver.
<Voct> I have this idea
<Voct> there's an interdimensional agency that sends out missions to deliver emergency supplies to soldiers in need
<Voct> wherever they might be
<Voct> an enlisted man is an enlisted man, no matter whose army he's in
<Voct> and
<Voct> they recruit their pilots from the armies of the dimensions where they make their supply drops
<Voct> and
<Voct> they're very careful to never send a pilot back into his own timeline
<Voct> because if he's in his own timeline, he'll be tempted to play favorites
<VAE> how do they handle recruiting. Forcible, or voluntary
<Voct> You've just been shot at. The bullet is coming right at your forehead. We've stopped time to make you this offer.
<Voct> You can join our benevolent organization
<Voct> in which case, we will take you out of your life and you will never see this timeline again
<Voct> or we can let you meet the death that's coming to you anyway.
<Voct> and you can have an honorable soldier's death in your own timeline.
<Voct> is that forcible recruitment, or voluntary?
<VAE> voluntary x3
<Voct> anyway. They've sent out a pilot in an RAF plane
<Voct> given him an Englishman's cover identity
<Voct> and some instalearning of Englishman vocabulary
<Voct> sure, the vocabulary isn't perfect, but it's not like he's going to land and have a conversation wiht anyone
<VAE> right. So the repetition is kinda because he's a foreigner to english
<VAE> and is going "Right, that's a neat way to say this" in his head
<Voct> yeah
<Voct> and grabbing useful phrases from Dr Lionel's speech
<Voct> and then when he gets to the dead serious meat of the conversatiion
<Voct> I mean, look at stuff like this
<Voct> I do not often visit the same location more than once
<Voct> you fire the flare, I bring in the supplies, I drop them. That is how my missions always work.
<Voct> How long is irrelevant. A flare is fired, and I answer, old chap. I have delivered supplies for many.
<Voct> and when he talks about his superiors
<Voct> it all makes perfect sense
<VAE> In a way, it does, supposing that his job is to come out every time
<VAE> "hey, this is a clever way we can get rid of the nonfunctional crap"
<VAE> bonus points that the stuff isn't completely useless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is where the write-up really came together for me. Norwood isn't a ghost, Norwood is some kind of imperfect bot.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: during the war, it was king and country.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I did it for queen and country, chap.
Jolly good show, old bean!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how about this?
website_of_planecrash
“an ordinary plane of the same type.
photograph taken in 19██“

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what this reminds me of?

1848 and 1377.

Something is just not right about it, and it is trying SO MUCH to be authentic and fit in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But the mangled stereotype is part of the charm of it. It's not an author making that mess through cluelessness, but intentionally done to clue in the reader that the voice isn't coming from something human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept but I could do without the mangled British stereotype. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the reasoning for the flawed supplies made perfect sense. It's the sort of punishment someone would come up with in a strict bureaucracy: they're expressing their irritation at the researchers who are wasting their time, while still following the apparent rules, and without actually causing a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The radio log makes this. +1 at "What else do you try to figure out, old chap?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd recently commented (I think on the absurd magical zoo scp) that I'm really tired of the "excuse to write a catalogue of wacky anomalous items" SCPs, but this actually puts the items in a context that makes them interesting. The whole ghost-story aspect of this (horrendous Britishisms aside) is so good it makes me angry. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyable SCP. I have to say on a personal note I would have preferred the items dropped to have been a bit more interesting, or at least the reasoning behind the items being inferior to have a bit more explanation, but I think I can just put that down to personal preference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the supplies actually went through several revisions.

First they were super useful magi-tech supplies that were so totally perfect at what they did.

Then they were more normal, but pretty great.

Then they were normal.

Then they were slightly wrong.

Then they were useless.

They got worse with every edit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Want to know more?
Well here's a secret:

Norwood is actually SCP-682. From the future. Bet you didn't see that coming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to know, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, there was also going to be a photo of the plane, but I couldn't find any 685 Avro York photos that were;

A) In color
B) In flight
and
C) Not clearly close to the ground with buildings in the background

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Righto Govna, this was quite the sticky wicket at first, but you and I gave it a swift kick in the bum, just like those blasted Jerries during the war!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much obliged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank Voct for the ridiculous part of things, I was originally a lot more subdued with it. Thanks though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately it'd be very, very easy to do that wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Figure out what you don't like?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Predictably? Nonsense?

And you're not downvoting?

I confess to being confused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is it nonsense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thing pops up predictably, spouts a bunch of nonsense, then disappears? I guess I can't really downvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, but I really dislike something about this. I'm not sure what it is, yet, so I'm not going to vote. I'll think about it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My sentiments exactly. The uncanny-valley British pilot… thing… also worked surprisingly well. That log really gels this for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, especially now the "bad accent" angle has been thrown out there. It wouldn't be too hard to make an audio file for the radio log, a bit of static and a propeller noise for effect, somebody doing a barely passable and clichéd accent for the "ghost" and an SCP operator with a monotone, clinical voice (as how I image the voices of SCP researchers to sound.)
Hopefully coming down on the right side of creepy if done right, done wrong and it could sound cringeworthy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Um. Wow. Um. Wow.

My first thought was, "A gun at +42?" After reading the description, it was easy to see why it wasn't negative, and after reading the interview log, easy to see why it's so high. It's Lord Blackwood as Mister Deeds in an airplane. That whole thing just completely blindsided me and I am absolutely loving the ridiculousness of Norwood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe 'ol Norwood is the supply guy for the delegates and/or the doorstep babies. Or possibly for their maker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see it as teasing, no. Norwood is not at all happy with the Foundation and their silly, pointless, time-wasting experiments.

Although — "as one Englishman to another" ?

Dr Lionel doesn't have the slightest trace of an English accent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Skipping Stone 23

Skipping

Ahhhh, I see what you did there!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even better/worse, it's not trying to hide it. It fully acknowledges that it's not quite human. And is it just me, or is Norwood teasing Dr. Lionel: "You tell me you're classified? Then so am I, old chap."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Either that, or it's what little is left of the soul of the old pilot, so faded and twisted that even its mannerism and accent are a crude parody of what it's supposed to be. It works either way, in my opinion, as does the article as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that whatever this thing is is doing a horrible mangled interpretation of a British accent is actually quite frightening. Like, it could have been just a ghost plane, but now it's something trying to imitate a human and not quite getting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Top-hole! Bally Jerry pranged his kite right in the how's-your-father! Hairy blighter dicky-birdied, feathered back on his Sammy, took a waspy, flipped over on his Betty Harper's and caught his can in the Bertie.

(Upvoted.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. Up voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel bad as well.
That gun photo was so so boring that I completely skipped over this article the first time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was honestly a little worried when I saw a gun in the picture, but everything turned out better than expected. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The audio log really clinched it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I love how the guy's dialogue is so repetitive; it sounds like a toy that only has a few stock phrases.

It's one of those SCPs that leaves me in the dark as to what the hell it is, but gives enough clues that I want to learn more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… i like it for the vague, maybe not even there, Cargo Cult reference. But it ticks for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it mostly for the astoundingly… something logic bomb drops in the interview. They aren't getting real supplies because they don't need real supplies, and they're not getting a real Englishman either. Rockin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mostly Voct on that, my draft was far more subtle about it and apparently gave more of an impression of a weird British ghost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah… it reads as someone/something trying too hard to sound Brit- *suddenly gets it*… Well played, Harland…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And here I was worried that it would come across as too comical.

Dammit, Voct wins again.
